stories.title: Hungary pipeline false-flag claims swirl as JD Vance makes election intervention
stories.summary: Claims explosives found near pipeline come before election in which PM Viktor Orbán is trailing in most polls Hungary has placed the gas pipeline that straddles the Serbian border under military protection, the prime minister, Viktor Orbán, has said, as accusations of a false-flag operation continued to swirl before a crunch election at the weekend and an official visit on Tuesday from the US vice-president, JD Vance. Orbán travelled to Hungary’s southern border with Serbia on Monday, one day af

stories.title: Democrats accuse ICE of creating ‘disappearances’ on US soil
stories.summary: Lawmakers led by Elizabeth Warren in scathing letter say system used to track detainees ‘increasingly unreliable’ A group of 36 lawmakers says the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) has created “disappearances” on US soil, due to the “increasingly unreliable” online system used to track people detained by immigration authorities, according to a letter shared with the Guardian. The lawmakers, led by Senators Elizabeth Warren, are urging that the DHS inspector general’s office open an investiga

stories.title: From ‘stink bugs’ to ‘enemies of the people’: how Viktor Orbán blazed a trail for Trump’s media assaults | Amrit Singh
stories.summary: Hungary’s prime minister has conducted a systematic attack on independent media. The parallels with the US are chilling During his state of the nation address earlier this year, Hungary’s prime minister, Viktor Orbán, outlined a chilling vision of the country’s future. Signalling a new level of aggression in his campaign against the truth if he is returned to power in elections on 12 April, Orbán vowed to purge the country of “bought journalists” and “fake civil society organisations”. Media rep

stories.title: DHS shutdown enters eighth week as Mike Johnson faces Republican revolt over funding deal – live
stories.summary: The partial government shutdown has now lasted eight weeks; Markwayne Mullin considers pulling US customs agents from airports in sanctuary cities During a press conference in Budapest with Hungary’s prime minister Viktor Orbán , vice-president JD Vance is asked how the military goals in Iran can be achieved if the US continues its attacks on the country. Vance was also asked about reports about US attacks on Kharg Island. The vice-president said the plan was to hit “some military targets” there

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy reiterates truce offer ahead of Orthodox Easter
stories.summary: Ukrainian president says Russia unlikely to accept – ‘for them, nothing is sacred’; Australian police arrest army reservist for joining war. What we know on day 1,504 Ukraine’s president has renewed his offer to Russia of a mutual ceasefire on strikes against energy infrastructure . “If Russia is ready to stop strikes on our energy infrastructure, we will respond in kind,” he said. “This proposal has been conveyed to the Russian side through the Americans.” Volodymyr Zelenskyy offered last week

stories.title: JD Vance slams Brussels ‘bureaucrats’ for meddling in Hungary before election
stories.summary: BUDAPEST — U.S. Vice President JD Vance on Tuesday insisted that Brussels rather than Washington was interfering in Hungarian politics before a crunch election on April 12, which will decide the future of key MAGA ally Prime Minister Viktor Orbán. Vance’s criticism of the EU came amid a full-throated endorsement of Orbán, although he denied he was trying to boost the flagging campaign of the Kremlin-aligned leader, who has run Hungary for the past 16 years. “Now, I don’t expect, of c

stories.title: Serbia looks for pipeline sabotage plot culprit as Vance's Hungary visit nears
stories.summary: Serbia has opened a probe into the attempted pipeline sabotage after explosives were found near Kanjiža, as military intelligence suspects a migrant with training, raising the temperature before a key election in Hungary on Sunday.

stories.title: Viktor Orbán told Putin ‘I am at your service’ in October phone call
stories.summary: Transcript reportedly details Hungarian leader offering whatever assistance he can to his Russian counterpart Europe live – latest updates Hungary elections explainer Hungary’s Viktor Orbán offered to go to great lengths to help Vladimir Putin, telling the Russian leader “I am at your service” in an October call, it has emerged, prompting further scrutiny of Budapest’s ties to the Kremlin just as JD Vance arrived in the city. Air Force Two landed in Budapest on Tuesday morning carrying the US vi

stories.title: Hungary: US dispatches JD Vance to aid Orban reelection bid
stories.summary: The US is deploying Vice President JD Vance to stump for longtime Hungarian PM Viktor Orban who trails in election polls. Orban is a symbol of the global far-right and an inspiration for Donald Trump.

stories.title: JD Vance arrives in Hungary to back Orbán's re-election bid
stories.summary: The US vice-president's visit is the latest show of White House support for the Hungarian leader.

stories.title: Orbán’s rival Magyar slams JD Vance’s Hungary visit as election meddling
stories.summary: Hungarian opposition leader Péter Magyar on Tuesday accused the U.S. of meddling in the country’s upcoming election, hitting out at Vice President JD Vance’s visit to Budapest just days before voters head to the polls. “No foreign country may interfere in Hungarian elections. This is our country,” Magyar wrote on X , adding that Hungarian history “is not written in Washington, Moscow, or Brussels.” Vance arrived Tuesday for talks with Prime Minister Viktor Orbán and a public address, in what Bud

stories.title: Vance accuses EU of ‘foreign interference’ in upcoming Hungarian election while endorsing Orbán – Europe live
stories.summary: US vice-president claims ‘the bureaucrats in Brussels have tried to destroy the economy of Hungary’ … and here they are! JD Vance and Usha Vance off the Air Force Two, welcomed by Hungarian foreign minister Péter Szijjártó as they begin their two-day trip to the Hungarian capital. Continue reading...

stories.title: House Democrats demand end to ‘cruel’ US energy blockade after visit to Cuba
stories.summary: Pramila Jayapal and Jonathan Jackson denounce ‘collective punishment’ amid vast disruption s from US oil blockade Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Two Democratic US lawmakers on Monday called for an end to the “cruel collective punishment” of Cuba after they visited the island to witness the effects of an US energy blockade. The US House members Pramila Jayapal of Washington and Jonathan Jackson of Illinois met with the Cuban president, Miguel Díaz-Ca

stories.title: Hungarian Easter tradition sees women doused with water in Hollókő
stories.summary: A centuries-old Easter tradition continues in a Hungarian village, where men pour water over women in a ritual rooted in folklore and springtime customs.

stories.title: Serbia, Hungary say explosives found at Russian gas pipeline
stories.summary: Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic said detonators and explosives "of devastating power" were found near the Balkan Stream pipeline, which runs through Serbia and provides Russian gas to Hungary.

stories.title: Elemző: Szakadék szélén áll a Fidesz, és az RMDSZ is ott van mellette
stories.summary: A Fidesz rendszerének megingása nemcsak Magyarországon, hanem Erdélyben is érezteti a hatását. Kiss Tamás szociológus szerint az RMDSZ olyan helyzetbe sodródott, ahol már nincs igazán jó választás.

stories.title: Orbán Magyar Péternél egy órával később zárja a kampányt szombaton
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök Budapesten, a Tisza Párt elnöke Debrecenben mond beszédet szombat este.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor elengedte a kormányt
stories.summary: De nem azt.

stories.title: Félnapos élő műsorban mutatjuk be J. D. Vance érkezését
stories.summary: Itt lesz velünk Buda Péter biztonságpolitikai elemző, Győri Gábor Amerika-szakértő, a Political Capital szakértői, de Orbán Viktor és J. D. Vance megszólalása mellett azt is megmutatjuk, hogyan rúgja be a hetet Magyar Péter.

stories.title: Magyar Péter reggel 7-kor indította a kampány utolsó hetét Ráckevén, Török Gábor a körzet jelentőségéről ír
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt elnöke újra a Ricsi nevű platós kocsi tetejéről beszélt, videóin az látszott, hogy a HÉV-állomáson sokan gyűltek össze.

stories.title: Volt, hogy rossz minőségű követ szállított a Budapest–Belgrádhoz Orbán apjának cége
stories.summary: Jegyzőkönyvek tanúskodnak arról, hogy nem mindig felelt meg az előírásoknak a Gántról érkező zúzott kő.

stories.title: Annyira nem veszi komolyan a pénzügyi világ a szerbiai robbanószerek ügyét, hogy összeomlás helyett pozitívban nyitott a budapesti tőzsde
stories.summary: A befektetők beárazták a szerb–magyar határ közelében talált hátizsákok által jelentett veszélyt.

stories.title: Leszállt Budapesten J. D. Vance, az Egyesült Államok alelnöke
stories.summary: Az amerikai alelnököt Szijjártó Péter fogadta, aki arról beszélt, 35 éve nem volt rá példa, hogy „ilyen magas rangú amerikai vendéget üdvözölhettünk”.

stories.title: Bloomberg: Orbán azt mondta Putyinnak, kész bármit megtenni, mint a kisegér az oroszlánnak
stories.summary: A lap egy októberi telefonhívásról szerzett meg jegyzőkönyvet, amiben Orbán magát egérhez, az orosz vezetőt pedig oroszlánhoz hasonlította.

stories.title: Magyar Péter szerint ha valódi terrorveszély lenne, lemondták volna az amerikai alelnök látogatását
stories.summary: Magyar Péter szerint a szerbiai gázvezeték elleni támadáskísérlet „harmadosztályú operett-előadás volt”.

stories.title: Meghalt Madarász Katalin népdal- és nótaénekes
stories.summary: A magyar nóta és a cigány dal egyik legnépszerűbb előadója 92 éves volt.

stories.title: J.D. Vance arrives in Budapest
stories.summary: The Vice President of the United States was welcomed by Minister of Foreign Affairs and Trade, Péter Szijjártó, who previously noted that "it has been 35 years since “we had welcomed such a high-ranking American guest.”

stories.title: Keressük Szalay-Bobrovniczky Kristóf honvédelmi minisztert, hogy kérdéseket tegyünk fel neki a Magyar Honvédség helyzetéről
stories.summary: A miniszter eddig csak Facebookon reagált Pálinkás Szilveszter százados állításaira, nyilvános fórumon nem jelent meg, ahol újságírók feltehetnék neki a fontos kérdéseket. Ha tudja, merre jár a honvédelmi miniszter, írja meg nekünk!

stories.title: Kijöttek az uniós adatok: ismét Magyarországon drágultak legnagyobb mértékben a lakások
stories.summary: Térképen, melyik országban mekkora volt az áremelkedés.

stories.title: A kegyes oroszlán nem falja fel a nyavalyás férgecske egeret a mesében, amit Orbán idézhetett Putyinnak
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök valószínűleg Ezópusz fabulájára gondolt, amikor az orosz elnökkel telefonált, de az is lehet, hogy a Tolsztoj-féle, kegyesebb változatra gondolt.

stories.title: Szégyenletes, hogy milyen mértékben avatkozik be a brüsszeli bürokrácia a magyar választásba – Vance a látogatása okáról
stories.summary: Vance azt mondta, az Orbánt sakkban tartó EU-nak akart üzenni a látogatásával. Élő közvetítés és elemzés Vance látogatásáról és az elmúlt napok legfontosabb fejleményeiről.

stories.title: Republikon Intézet: Magyar Pétert többen tartják alkalmasabbnak a miniszterelnöki posztra
stories.summary: Az Orbán alkalmassága mellett voksolók viszont magabiztosabbak a válaszukban.

stories.title: Lázár címmel írták meg a magyarországi mágnások hanyatlástörténetét
stories.summary: A családi múltba vezető versek és regények, valamint a szentföldi svédek között megesett csodák. E heti könyvajánlónkban az első női irodalmi Nobel-díjas és a bűnregény hazai felforgatójának műve is szerepel.

stories.title: Vance szerint bárkivel együttműködnek majd a választás után, de az a tervük, hogy Orbán Viktor nyer
stories.summary: Orbán azt mondta az amerikai alelnökkel közös sajtótájékoztatón, hogy Magyarország továbbra is kész helyt adni egy Moszkva–Washington közti találkozónak. Vance szerint ami Magyarországon történt, az az egyik legrosszabb példája a külföldi beavatkozásnak. Vance pont kapott egy üzenetet Trump közel-keleti követétől, Witkofftól a sajtótájékoztató közben.

stories.title: A nagy drágulás nem tett jót a tojásfogyasztásnak
stories.summary: A drasztikus áremelkedés eredményeként jelentősen csökkent az elmúlt években Magyarországon az egy főre jutó tojásfogyasztás.

stories.title: Beperli az államot Bige László, szerinte Lázár miatt nem kaptak hatósági engedélyt
stories.summary: A Nitrogénművek Zrt. elnök-vezérigazgatója szerint saját forrásból 10 milliárdos beruházást hajtottak végre, de lassan egy éve várják a hatósági engedélyt, hogy használhassák az üzemet.

stories.title: Titokban állami tízmilliárdokat pumpáltak Orbán egyik kedvenc cégébe, amely aztán később hadiipari cégeket vett az államtól
stories.summary: A Direkt36 birtokába került dokumentumok szerint rejtett módon kerültek állami források a 4iG nevű céghez. Ez épp abban az időben történt, amikor a kormány úgy döntött, hogy ennek a vállalatnak adnak el állami védelmi cégeket.

stories.title: „Készen állok arra, hogy azonnal segítsek” – itt a Putyin–Orbán beszélgetés teljes leirata
stories.summary: A magyar miniszterelnök tavaly októberben a Budapestre tervezett amerikai–orosz találkozó kapcsán beszélt az orosz elnökkel, a Bloomberg szerezte meg a beszélgetés leiratát.

stories.title: Mexican art world protests over plan to send Frida Kahlo masterpieces to Spain
stories.summary: Cultural figures sign open letter asking government for clarity on how long landmark collection will remain abroad One of the world’s most important collections of 20th-century Mexican art, including works by Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera, is set to be exported to Spain under an agreement with Banco Santander , sparking outrage among Mexico’s cultural community. Nearly 400 cultural professionals have signed an open letter calling on the Mexican government to offer greater clarity on what the deal

stories.title: Poland's border with Belarus sees decrease in illegal immigration attempts by 96%
stories.summary: The Polish government has announced a significant decrease in the number of attempts to illegally cross the border with Belarus, pointing to the effectiveness of its migration policy and the reinforced activities of its services.

stories.title: In Estonia, rumors are circulating that the city of Narva wants to secede and join Russia. Is it true?
stories.summary: The Estonian city of Narva is on Europe's eastern border, right next to Russia. Recently there have been online rumors that some locals want to secede from Estonia and join Russia. What's really happening?

stories.title: German XXL frigate on the horizon - but when will the super warship arrive?
stories.summary: Germany does not have an aircraft carrier, but is planning the largest frigate in the world - the F126. It is due to set sail in 2029 and will be suitable for submarine hunting or drone operations, depending on the mission. But there are problems with the vessel. When will the super ship arrive?

stories.title: Hungary's government accused of spying on opposition
stories.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban's government used the country's intelligence service to spy on the opposition Tisza Party, says investigative platform Direkt36. What does this mean for the upcoming parliamentary election?

stories.title: News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar

stories.title: ‘Vile on every level’: Tucker Carlson rips Donald Trump over Easter Sunday ‘f-word’ post
stories.summary: Conservative pundit  Tucker Carlson tore into Donald Trump  on Monday night, calling an Easter Sunday social media post from the U.S. president “vile on every level” and accusing him of threatening to commit a war crime. “How dare you speak that way on Easter morning to the country?” Carlson said in a monologue on his podcast. “Who do you think you are? You’re tweeting out the f-word on Easter morning.” On Sunday, a major Christian holiday, Trump posted a profane message on Truth Socia

stories.title: Trump threatens ‘whole civilization will die tonight’ ahead of Iran deadline
stories.summary: President Donald Trump threatened the death of an entire civilization in a Tuesday morning post on Truth Social, the latest violent warning for the Iranian regime ahead of a deadline for  reopening the Strait of Hormuz set to expire at 8 p.m . Tuesday night may well be “one of the most important moments in the long and complex history of the World,”  he wrote . “A whole civilization will die tonight, never to be brought back again,” he wrote. “I don’t want that to happen, but it probab

stories.title: The Stranger review – lustrously beautiful and superbly realised modern take on the Camus classic
stories.summary: François Ozon’s adaptation of the 1942 novella L’Etranger passionately honours the original text while bringing a contemporary perspective to its themes of empire and race A heatstricken reverie of violence and mystery unfolds in this film, a numb ecstasy of the inexplicable, as experienced by a sensitive white European under the unbearable noonday sun. Set in 1940s French Algeria (and filmed in Morocco), François Ozon’s lustrously beautiful and superbly realised monochrome version of Albert Cam

stories.title: Israel warns Iranians not to use trains as Trump deadline looms
stories.summary: Threat comes after US president said he would bomb power plants and bridges if Iran did not reopen strait of Hormuz Middle East crisis – live updates Israel has warned Iranians their lives will be at risk if they use the country’s railways on Tuesday before the end of a negotiations deadline imposed by Donald Trump with a threat to destroy Iran’s bridges and power plants. stories.title: Trump says ‘a whole civilisation will die tonight’ if Iran does not make a deal – Middle East crisis live
stories.summary: The US president once again warned Iran to make a deal to avert threat of massive attacks Here are some of the latest images coming in from the Middle East as the war continues in week six. The Israeli military has just warned the people of Iran not to use trains, saying that doing so “endangers your life” . stories.title: Iran still refuses to reopen Strait of Hormuz as Trump threatens infrastructure strikes
stories.summary: The Tehran regime has pushed back on the US president's ultimatum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, as Trump again reiterated his threat of Iran's "complete demolition" by the end of his deadline.

stories.title: Russia provided Iran with intelligence on Israeli energy sites, Ukraine says
stories.summary: Kyiv stated that Moscow is sharing its satellite intelligence with Iran to help Tehran strike Israel’s energy sites, which could cause power grid collapse and mass blackouts.

stories.title: My NATO rage began with Greenland, Trump says
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said Monday evening that his recent grievances with NATO trace back to a standoff earlier this year over Greenland. “It all began with, if you want to know the truth, Greenland,” Trump said during a White House press conference. “We want Greenland. They don’t want to give it to us. And I said, ‘bye, bye.’” The remark revives a dispute that rattled transatlantic ties in January, when Trump threatened to take control of the self-ruling Danish territory before backing do

stories.title: Gunmen open fire near Israeli consulate building in Istanbul
stories.summary: Three gunmen were involved in a shootout with police near the Israeli consulate in Istanbul on Tuesday, leaving one attacker dead and two wounded and in custody, Turkish authorities said. One of the attackers had links to an “organization that exploits religion,” said Turkish Minister of the Interior Mustafa Çiftçi in a statement . He later specified that the three “terrorists” had been “neutralized” during the clash with the police, with one suspect being kil

stories.title: ‘Italy has the best benefits’: Milan takes on Dubai as home for the super-rich
stories.summary: With the UAE under fire from Iranian missiles, wealthy investors are turning to Italy’s flat-tax haven Just over a month ago, Dubai was the obvious destination for wealthy Britons in search of a new home. Few cities allow you to earn vast sums tax-free and spend them across any number of luxury hotels, restaurants and shops. But as the United Arab Emirates comes under Iranian fire , Dubai’s reputation – in part created by emigrant influencers – as a haven for the global elite is eroding. Super-r

stories.title: Talks to end Iran war appear to falter a day before Trump deadline

stories.title: Ben Roberts-Smith arrested: former Australian soldier charged with five war crime murders in Afghanistan

stories.title: Europe mulls the prospect of a NATO without the US
stories.summary: As the US president escalates threats to destroy Iran, will NATO be collateral damage? A former American ambassador says the alliance is “not dead” but “deeply damaged” by transatlantic divisions.

stories.title: Taiwan opposition leader visits China for expected meeting with Xi Jinping
stories.summary: Cheng Li-wun said she "gladly accepted" Xi Jinping's invitation and hopes to be a "bridge for peace".

stories.title: Russian strike on Odesa kills three, including child
stories.summary: A Russian overnight attack on Ukraine’s port city of Odesa killed three people, including a child, and left several others injured, authorities said.

stories.title: Russian drone strike on bus stop in central Ukraine kills three and injures 12
stories.summary: The Russian drone targeted a moving bus deliberately as it was pulling in to a stop to allow waiting passengers on board, officials said.

stories.title: Trump says 'whole civilisation will die tonight' if Iran ultimatum expires
stories.summary: Previously, the US president has warned that unless Tehran reopened the Strait of Hormuz, the US would unleash what he called the "complete demolition" of Iran's critical infrastructure overnight on Wednesday.

stories.title: 'We're sinking deeper': Iranians brace for infrastructure strikes as Trump deadline nears
stories.summary: Ordinary Iranians respond to the US president's threat to destroy Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran warns of ‘devastating’ retaliation after Trump’s expletive-laden threats over strait of Hormuz

A spokeswoman for Mitsui O.S.K. Lines told AFP that the Green Asha – a liquefied petroleum gas tanker – had crossed the waterway. stories.title: Spanish politicians clash over request to move Picasso’s Guernica
stories.summary: Madrid and Basque government leaders call each other ‘provincial’ in dispute over the artwork A row has broken out between the Madrid and Basque regional governments in Spain over the latter’s request for Guernica, probably Picasso’s most celebrated work, to be housed temporarily in the Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao to mark the 90th anniversary of the bombing of the Basque town. The work has hung in the Reina Sofía museum in Madrid since 1992 and repeated requests for it to be moved to the Basque

stories.title: Bangladesh launches measles vaccination drive as child death toll passes 100
stories.summary: UN assists in emergency vaccination drive as country battles worst surge in cases in years amid fall in vaccination rates Bangladesh is battling its worse measles outbreak in years, with more than 100 children dead amid a rise in unvaccinated infants. The government, in partnership with the United Nations, has begun conducting an emergency measles-rubella vaccination drive for children across the country, after more than 900 cases were confirmed since March. Continue reading...

stories.title: US is ‘using Mexico as a garbage sink’ leading to ‘toxic crisis’, UN expert says
stories.summary: Marcos Orellana, a special rapporteur, found lax environmental standards and lack of oversight allowed pollution to accumulate Mexico is facing a “toxic crisis” and has become a “garbage sink” for the US, exposing Mexican communities to dangerous pollution, a UN expert has warned. In an interview with the Guardian and Quinto Elemento Lab, an investigative outlet, Marcos Orellana, an environmental specialist, said pollutants ranging from imported waste to dangerous pesticides are affecting people

stories.title: ‘There’s a lot of desperation’: skilled older workers turn to AI training to stay afloat
stories.summary: They have degrees, expertise and years of experience – but can’t find work. For many Americans, AI training has become a last refuge in a brutal job market When Patrick Ciriello lost his job and couldn’t find work for nearly a year, his family’s foundation crumbled. “You hear about people who hit rock bottom,” Ciriello told the Guardian. “Well, I was there.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Albanese brings forward Singapore trip and speaks with China in bid to shore up petrol shipments to Australia

stories.title: UK Jewish groups threaten protests if Kanye West’s Wireless shows go ahead
stories.summary: Campaign Against Antisemitism calls on rapper to cancel gigs, while rapper offers to meet Jewish representatives Kanye West will face mass protests if his three-night residency at London’s Wireless festival goes ahead, according to Jewish groups who say that if the rapper is truly sorry for his antisemitic remarks he should cancel this summer’s gigs. In a statement on Tuesday, West, who is legally known as Ye, offered to “meet and listen” to members of the UK’s Jewish community after a backlash

stories.title: Scientists develop gene-edited wheat that can make toasted bread less carcinogenic
stories.summary: Bread and biscuits made from Crispr-edited wheat showed substantially reduced acrylamide levels Scientists have developed gene-edited wheat that can be used to make bread that is less carcinogenic when toasted. Researchers at Rothamsted Research in Harpenden, Hertfordshire, used Crispr genome editing, which allows researchers to selectively edit the DNA of living organisms. This technology was adapted for use in the laboratory from naturally occurring genome editing systems found in bacteria. Co

stories.title: JP Morgan reaches agreement with City airport for Canary Wharf’s tallest tower
stories.summary: Bank to submit planning application shortly after securing deal on 265m-high London Docklands building Business live – latest updates JP Morgan Chase has reached agreement with London City airport to build one of Europe’s tallest office towers in the east of the capital. The £3bn tower is poised to be the tallest in the Canary Wharf financial district, after JP Morgan, one of Wall Street’s biggest banks, secured approval from the airport. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK government bans Kanye West from entering country
stories.summary: Rapper was booked to play at Wireless festival in London, prompting outcry over his past antisemitic remarks UK politics live – latest updates The rapper formerly known as Kanye West has been banned from entering the UK amid a deepening political row over his previous antisemitic statements. West, who is legally known as Ye, made an application to travel to the UK via an Electronic Travel Authorisation on Monday but it has been blocked by officials. Continue reading...

stories.title: Video undermines ICE account of officer shooting a man in Minneapolis

The video, from a city-owned security camera, captured federal officers chasing one of the men to his residence. Another Venezuelan man who lives there was shot during the confrontation, which e

stories.title: Greek PM vows to tackle ‘deep state’ in wake of farm fraud scandal
stories.summary: Kyriakos Mitsotakis calls alleged scamming of EU agricultural funds ‘a turning point’ The Greek prime minister has vowed to tackle what he has called a “deep state” he says is plaguing the country, as he sought to address a growing political crisis over a farm fraud scandal that has forced the resignation of multiple government ministers. In a speech, aired on national TV, Kyriakos Mitsotakis attempted to limit the damage, describing the revelations as “a turning point” that had turbo-charged hi

stories.title: Artemis II breaks record for farthest human spaceflight
stories.summary: The Orion spacecraft is in its final phase, with NASA saying astronauts have broken Apollo 13's record for traveling the farthest distance from Earth.

stories.title: Vietnam elects Communist Party leader To Lam as president
stories.summary: A China-style mandate for To Lam marks a shift in Vietnam's power structure where party leadership and state presidency were previously held by different people.

stories.title: Australia arrests decorated veteran Ben Roberts-Smith on war crimes charges
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith is a Victoria Cross recipient and had been linked to the murder of unarmed civilians while deployed in Afghanistan.

stories.title: Can small nuclear reactors solve Europe's energy woes?
stories.summary: Supporters say modular reactors are key to meeting energy goals, but critics say they're a costly distraction.

stories.title: Taiwan opposition leader heads to China for 'peace'
stories.summary: Kuomintang's chairwoman Cheng Li-wun hopes to meet Xi Jinping during her visit to China. Her goal, she said, is to "to preserve peace is to preserve Taiwan."

stories.title: Iran war: Tehran defiant as Trump deadline nears
stories.summary: Tehran says it wants a permanent end to the war but refused to fully open the Strait of Hormuz, despite President Donald Trump warning it to meet his demands or be "taken out." Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Germany news: Minister floats fare-dodging decriminalization
stories.summary: A debate is brewing about whether fare-evasion on trams, buses and trains in Germany should no longer be a criminal offense. Meanwhile, companies are starting to feel the pinch of war in the Middle East. DW has more.

stories.title: India news: Nuclear reactor in Kalpakkam attains criticality
stories.summary: India is advancing its civil nuclear program with its Fast Breeder Reactor at Kalpakkam now able to self-sustain a nuclear chain reaction. In other news, nine police officers have been sentenced to death. DW has more.

stories.title: Why have US-South Africa relations soured?
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump wants to sideline South Africa on the world stage. What is his issue with the country and when did things start going south?

stories.title: One in five young Germans plans to leave the country
stories.summary: A recent study shows that as many as one in five Germans between the ages of 14 and 29 are actively thinking about moving to another country — and 40% plan to do so in the long term.

stories.title: Inside India's courts, AI's growing role sparks concern
stories.summary: The increasing use of artificial intelligence in courtrooms raises worries that the technology may aggravate bias and structural distortions in the legal system.

stories.title: US citizens beat rising energy bills with homegrown power

stories.title: South Korea turns to EU to revive talks with the North
stories.summary: Seoul's unification minister has urged Brussels to act as mediator in North-South discussions, but Pyongyang still seems to show little appetite for interaction.

stories.title: North Korea welcomes Seoul regret over drones as 'wise'

stories.title: Congo joins US list of third-country deportation destinations

stories.title: China is winning one AI race, the US another - but either might pull ahead
stories.summary: Both sides don't want to let their rival dominate. And the competition may yet be transformed further.

stories.title: Easter festivities muted for Syrian Christians after recent violence
stories.summary: Syrian Christians saw their Easter celebrations confined to church prayers after armed groups attacked the town of Al-Suqaylabiyah in Hama on 27 March. Euronews spoke to the faithful in the country on one of Christianity's most important holidays.

stories.title: Locals dispute Nigerian army claim of 31 rescued after Easter attack

stories.title: UAE tells Euronews ceasefire not enough for Iran war solution, says 'no trust' in Tehran regime

stories.title: Australia's most-decorated living soldier charged over alleged war crimes

stories.summary: The anti-drone system, named after a mythical creature symbolising untamed forces of nature, uses electronic warfare technology to detect and neutralise UAVs without munitions.

stories.title: Pálinkás Szilveszter elmondta, hogyan próbálták rávenni, hogy ne álljon a nyilvánosság elé
stories.summary: Berendelte az irodájába Szalay-Bobrovniczky Kristóf, és három ajánlatot tett le az asztalra. A százados azt érezte, hogy a miniszter attól fél, hogy olyanok derülhetnek ki róla, amik rossz fényt vetnének rá és a minisztériumra. Pálinkás elmesélte, hogy mi történt vele az első interjú megjelenése óta, és milyen visszajelzéseket kapott a katonáktól.

stories.summary: Az észak-franciaországi balesetnek 13 sérültje van.

stories.title: Meloni in the Gulf: harmony on gas and defence with Qatar, the Emirates and Saudi Arabia

stories.title: We've gone mad for puzzles. This makes sense – it’s reassuring to have answers in these perplexing times | Joseph de Weck
stories.summary: Our world feels chaotic, confusing and unfair, but puzzles offer clear rules, solvable problems and reward for effort expended Maybe you’ve noticed it too. Everyone seems to have become fixated on puzzle games. In the morning, over coffee, I play Word Wheel on the Guardian app. Over lunch, colleagues compare notes on Tradle , the game where you guess a country from its exports. Which place exports about 45% fish and 50% crustaceans? Greenland. Another friend can’t fall asleep without her nightly

stories.title: When Suzuki met Suzuki: why a Tokyo dating agency is matching couples with the same name
stories.summary: Japan’s ban on married couples having different surnames has prompted an event to highlight people’s reluctance to change their name At the very least, the three men and three women calming their nerves on a Friday evening at a venue in Tokyo know they have one thing in common. Spaced out across booths, they will soon be placed in pairs and given 15 minutes to get to know one another. Continue reading...

stories.title: Weather tracker: Warm March in US leaves snowpack critically low

stories.title: Dozens of firms risk losing B Corp status after standards overhaul

stories.title: Dozens missing after migrant boat capsizes in Mediterranean
stories.summary: A boat departed from Libya carrying more than 100 people. Only 32 people are confirmed to have survived its voyage in the Mediterranean Sea.

stories.title: Russians living in exile cope with grief far from home
stories.summary: Since the start of the war against Ukraine, many Russians living in exile have been unable to return home for security reasons. Three of them told DW how they cope with the loss of loved ones far from their families.

stories.title: Mediterranean sharks are vanishing in a legal void
stories.summary: Along Libya's coast, sharks — including breeding females — are being sold unchecked. With little enforcement and growing financial pressure on fishermen, vulnerable species are spiraling toward extinction.

stories.title: Iran war suspends Easter church gatherings in Dubai, but Christians remain defiant against attacks
stories.summary: Due to safety concerns as Iran’s attacks continue, churches in Dubai suspended Easter in-person masses to avoid large gatherings. Catholic Christians in the UAE and the Gulf have embraced Easter’s message with hope and defiance amid the constant threat.

stories.title: More than 70 people feared dead after migrant boat capsizes in the central Mediterranean
stories.summary: A boat carrying around 105 people capsized in the central Mediterranean Sea after departing from Libya on Saturday. Two bodies were recovered and at least 70 are missing, according to NGOs Mediterranea Saving Humans and Sea-Watch.

stories.title: Israel strikes Iran's largest petrochemical facility as Trump's Hormuz deadline looms
stories.summary: Earlier on Monday, Iran warned of more devastating retaliation if the US hits civilian targets, as mediators pushed a 45-day ceasefire to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Revellers parade giant phalluses at Japan’s fertility festival
stories.summary: Crowds pack Kawasaki for Kanamara Matsuri, Japan’s colourful fertility festival using phallic shrines to celebrate life and challenge sex taboos.

stories.title: Going abroad: What will Germany's new military service act actually change
stories.summary: Under the new law, German men aged 17 to 45 must obtain authorisation for extended stays abroad. The obligation is not new, but critics say it should have been communicated more clearly.

stories.title: Ukraine and Syria to cooperate on security in unexpected military alliance
stories.summary: Ukraine's Zelenskyy arrived in Damascus in a surprise visit on Sunday, for his first official visit to Syria after the fall of the pro-Russian al-Assad regime.

stories.title: Mitsotakis urges swift EU probe into MPs over Greek farm subsidy fraud
stories.summary: Greek PM Kyriakos Mitsotakis has asked EU prosecutors to swiftly charge MPs over alleged €23m farm subsidy fraud, as resignations and pressure mount.

stories.title: Nasa’s Artemis II crew to reach unseen far side of the Moon on flyby
stories.summary: NASA’s Artemis II crew are expected to reach their destination on Monday where the four astronauts aboard the Orion will – for the first time – look at the lunar far side with the naked eye. The fly-by will last approximately six hours before the astronauts head back home.

stories.title: US and Iran race to find missing downed pilot as Lebanon faces intense Israeli strikes
stories.summary: The Israeli military have relaunched evacuation orders from the southern Lebanese city of Tyre, warning of more strikes, claiming they are targeting Hezbollah. Over 1.2 million people in Lebanon have been displaced since the beginning of the war.

stories.title: Starmer calls for ‘ambitious’ new UK-EU ties as Trump threatens to quit Nato
stories.summary: PM to focus on European defence and economic partnership for ‘dangerous world’, in pivot away from US UK politics live – latest updates The UK will seek an even deeper partnership with the EU because of the instability wreaked by Donald Trump’s war with Iran, Keir Starmer has said, adding that the moment called for a more ambitious deal with Brussels to strengthen trade and defence. His comments came as the US president again said he was considering pulling the US out of Nato , which he describe

stories.title: Young German men refusing military service
stories.summary: Military service remains voluntary in Germany. However, with the Bundeswehr needing more soldiers, conscription could soon return. But an increasing number of young men are choosing to opt out.

stories.title: German men need military permit for extended stays abroad
stories.summary: The new military service law requires all men under 45 to seek approval from the Bundeswehr to leave the country for longer than three months. It also obliges the military career center to issue it.

stories.title: Germany's new religious diversity
stories.summary: In Germany, many Christian churches are abandoned. But the number of Muslim, Jewish and Hindu buildings is increasing. Immigrants from India or Syria are making the urban landscape more diverse.

stories.title: New German conscription law: eligible men may now need approval for trips abroad
stories.summary: Men aged 17 to 45 may now need approval from the Bundeswehr for trips abroad lasting more than three months. What is behind the new military service law?

stories.title: Prayers, verses and holy books: How have religious symbols been used in the ongoing war in the Middle East?
stories.summary: The messages exchanged in the Middle East reflect a different face of warfare. Religious rhetoric is being employed in an uncommon and increasing way, with symbols of the three major monotheistic religions overlapping: Judaism, Christianity, and Islam in the middle of war.

stories.title: 'Absolutely spectacular': Artemis II crew see first glimpse of far side of Moon
stories.summary: Astronauts on the Artemis II mission are on the third day of their journey around the far side of the Moon.

stories.title: RFI Investigation: Ukrainian military in Libya and the strike on the Russian gas carrier Arctic Metagaz
stories.summary: French journalists report the presence of more than 200 Ukrainian military specialists in Libya and their alleged involvement in the attack on the Arctic Metagaz vessel using a maritime drone.

stories.title: Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement
stories.summary: Dozen people arrive under new deal but legal challenges expected with scheme criticised as ‘dehumanising process’ A flight carrying people being deported from the US has landed in Uganda, as Donald Trump’s administration pushes on with its strategy of expelling migrants to countries they have no ties to. The deported people would stay in the east African country as “a transition phase for potential onward transmission to other countries”, an unnamed senior Ugandan government official told Reuter

stories.title: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing appointed president after ‘sham’ election
stories.summary: Min Aung Hlaing seized control five years ago and plunged Myanmar into conflict and economic chaos Min Aung Hlaing, the military general who plunged Myanmar into conflict and economic chaos when he took power in the 2021 coup has been appointed president, months after widely condemned sham elections. Min Aung Hlaing, who is wanted by the prosecutor of the international criminal court for crimes against humanity against the Rohingya Muslim minority, was voted president by lawmakers on Friday. Mya

stories.title: Cubans study oil tanker diplomacy for signs of progress in secret talks with US
stories.summary: Despite hostile rhetoric Trump let a Russian ship break his blockade – could it herald a Venezuela-style outcome? When a sanctioned Russian oil tanker, the Anatoly Kolodkin, docked at Cuba’s Matanzas oil terminal on Tuesday, unloading 700,000 barrels of crude , it was not immediately clear why the ship had been allowed to pass through Donald Trump’s oil blockade. In January, the US president had proclaimed on social media: “THERE WILL BE NO MORE OIL OR MONEY GOING TO CUBA – ZERO!” yet last week

stories.title: Germany: Conservatives link immigration with crime
stories.summary: Will German citizens feel safer if more immigrants are deported? Chancellor Friedrich Merz has been giving that impression. But the reality is more complex.

stories.title: Pakistan and China propose five-part peace plan for Middle East
stories.summary: Foreign ministers Ishaq Dar and Wang Yi met in Beijing as Pakistan pushes for peacemaker role Middle East crisis – live updates Pakistan and China have released a joint five-part proposal for peace in the Middle East, after Pakistan’s foreign minister flew to Beijing on Tuesday to seek Chinese support for the country’s faltering efforts to negotiate an end to end the war. The one-day meeting between Ishaq Dar and his Chinese counterpart, Wang Yi, came as Pakistan continues to push for the role o

stories.title: People of Burkina Faso should forget about democracy, says military ruler
stories.summary: Ibrahim Traoré, who took power in 2022 coup, tells state broadcaster ‘we must tell the truth, democracy isn’t for us’ People in Burkina Faso should forget about democracy as it is “not for us”, the military president, Ibrahim Traoré, told the country’s state broadcaster. Traoré took power in a coup in September 2022 , toppling another junta that had taken power just nine months earlier. He has since stifled opposition and in January banned political parties outright. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘India is going to face a food crisis’: Farmers panic over fertiliser shortages amid Iran war
stories.summary: Ripple effects of oil and fertiliser shortage felt by farmers in India and Sri Lanka despite governments saying there is enough stock to go round Gurvinder Singh never thought the war in Iran would touch his quiet corner of Punjab. Yet looking out over his smallholding, where he alternates between wheat and rice crops in the state known as India’s breadbasket, the 52-year-old farmer can barely think of anything else. His anxiety over a conflict playing out thousands of miles away is crippling as

stories.title: Biometric checks stalled again for cross-Channel travellers
stories.summary: Fears of Easter chaos over scaling up of new EU border system are eased, with no facial IDs for Eurotunnel and Eurostar passengers Passengers crossing the Channel from the UK to France will not face new biometric checks in the coming weeks, despite an imminent deadline for the complete implementation of the EU’s entry-exit system (EES), ports say. Airlines and airports across Europe have feared chaos over the Easter holidays. Continue reading...

stories.title: Serbian local elections: Is Vucic's party losing its grip?
stories.summary: Serbia's ruling SNS once again secured victories in local elections — but sometimes just by a few hundred votes. Narrowing margins suggest a shifting political landscape and growing momentum for anti-government protests.

stories.title: Russian military plane crash in Crimea kills at least 29
stories.summary: The plane crashed in a mountainous forest area not far from Crimea's capital, Simferopol. Since the Kremlin sent troops into Ukraine, incidents involving Russian military planes have occurred frequently.

stories.title: 'Drill baby drill' ― Trump opens up nature to big energy
stories.summary: America's beloved national parks and public lands face conservation rollbacks and sell-offs as the administration of US President Donald Trump pushes for fossil fuel and timber extraction.

stories.title: Reeves criticises Trump for starting Iran war with no ‘clear plan’ to get out of it – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Starmer says he understands why people are concerned about the cost of living. He says he has already set out a five-point plan to deal with the crisis. Just look at what’s happening today. Today your energy bills will be cut because of the action that we took at the budget. And whatever happens in Iran, that price is now fixed until July. The most effective way we can support the cost of living in Britain is to push for de-escalation in the Middle East, and a reopening o

stories.title: One killed and buildings damaged as magnitude 7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia
stories.summary: Quake with epicentre west-north-west of Ternate island shakes cities and prompts regional tsunami warning One person has been killed after a 7.4 magnitude earthquake struck Indonesia’s Ternate island, damaging buildings and triggering small tsunami waves. The quake, which had a depth of 35km, occurred on Thursday at 6.48am local time, according to the United States Geological Survey. Its epicentre was 127km (79 miles) west-north-west of Ternate, an island in Indonesia’s North Maluku province. Co

stories.title: Crete skies turn eerie orange after Sahara dust storm – video
stories.summary: A Saharan dust storm blanketed the Mediterranean island of Crete as winds of up to force 9 on the Beaufort scale carried dust from north Africa, forcing several flights to be rerouted. The dust storm came as heavy rain and gale-force winds battered Greece, flooding dozens of homes and leading to at least one death Continue reading...

stories.title: Orange skies over Crete as Storm Erminio sweeps Saharan sand across Greek island – in pictures
stories.summary: Storm Erminio has caused widespread destruction in parts of Greece, including the Attica region where a man died in Nea Makri. On the island of Crete, skies turned an eerie orange as winds of up to force 9 on the Beaufort scale carried dust from North Africa, forcing several flights to be rerouted Continue reading...

stories.title: A day in the life of Asia’s fuel crisis
stories.summary: From farms in New Zealand to factories in Delhi, the effects of the oil crisis triggered by the Iran war are rippling across Asia Continue reading...

stories.title: Storm Erminio lashes much of Greece as Saharan dust blankets Crete
stories.summary: South-east Italy also affected by heavy rain, and snow at higher altitudes, while deadly flooding hits Afghanistan Parts of the Mediterranean have been lashed by Storm Erminio this week. Heavy rain, thunderstorms and occasional bursts of hail affected much of Greece throughout Wednesday and Thursday, with the most severe conditions across south-eastern parts of the mainland and several islands in the southern Aegean Sea, including Crete, with streets flooded and vehicles stranded. Some of the he

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Crisis in the Middle East, a Russian drone attack in Kharkiv, a Saharan dust storm in Crete and the launch of Artemis II – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Warning: this gallery contains images some readers may find distressing Continue reading...

stories.title: Architect of the Easter Rising, hanged as a traitor: for Roger Casement, a pardon still seems far away | Rory Carroll
stories.summary: Relations with Britain have improved again since Brexit, but battles over Irish history remain visible in Stormont’s endless feuding Rory Carroll is the Guardian’s Ireland correspondent and author of A Rebel and a Traitor: A Fugitive, the Manhunt and Birth of the IRA More than a century after he was marched to the gallows, there is still something radioactive about Roger Casement , a name that continues to emit a faint crackle in British-Irish relations. He was knighted in 1911 by King George V

stories.title: Tiger Woods 'stepping away' for treatment after DUI arrest
stories.summary: Tiger Woods has announced a break from golf four days after he crashed his SUV and was arrested for driving under influence. Fans had been awaiting his return at the Masters this month.
